block.title: Splunk Web Certificate Configuration
block.text: Setup your web certificate here. When you click save, the cert will be uploaded and Splunk will be configured to use the cert upon next restart.

input.label: Name your certificate here (Usually common name is good, ex. mysite.com)
Paste your certificate here including the begin/end header/footer
Paste your certificate's RSA key here including the begin/end header/footer

block.text: Notes: Clicking save will upload your cert and key to $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/myauth and create/modify $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/system/local/web.conf so that SSL will be enabled and the cert and key you provided will be used for the ssl. Nothing is set in stone until you restart but you can only manually revert the changes that will be made.
Troubleshooting: If for some reason Splunk Web doesnt start after the restart, check your $SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/splunkd.log for details and troubleshooting.

Caution: YOU WILL BE UPLOADING YOUR CERTIFICATE AND KEY OVER A NON-SSL CONNECTION IF YOU DONT ALREADY HAVE SSL ENABLED. THIS IS STUPID IN MANY CASESB BUT SAFE IN SOME. IF YOU DONT KNOW WHY IT WOULD BE STUPID, DONT PRESS SAVE. IF YOU AGREE IT'S STUPID THEN ENTER 'None' FOR YOUR KEY, AND EDIT THE KEY IN $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/myauth AFTER SAVING BUT BEFORE THE RESTART.
